title: Was my EA missing trades or just being selective?
author: Loose-Object-8913
selftext:
Been testing my EA the last couple weeks and noticed it barely traded during messy conditions.

At first thought it was missing moves, but turns out the higher timeframe filter was just too strict.

Tweaked it a bit and now it still avoids chop, but actually takes the cleaner setups.

Still refining it, but the “no trade” part has probably saved more than it’s missed.

Do you guys prefer more active systems or ones that just sit and wait?

Here’s my weekly update with new results: 

https://youtu.be/WktOZX0Ksdg?si=YnvZN8PJ-KdkBdzR

replies.author: Loose-Object-8913
replies.body: Fair enough. We all have different approaches. Do you prefer the martingale and grid EAs?

replies.author: Actual_Resort1892
replies.body: I prefer trend following algorithms personally. I used both grid and martingale systems before but it didn’t worked out well

replies.author: Loose-Object-8913
replies.body: Yeah me too. They too risky. This is why I designed something that doesn’t over trade and trade during chop. Easiest way to blow accounts is by allowing over trade during chop

comments.author: No_Independent8531
comments.body: If you can please answer what is the trade freq of your algo and what should be avg freq based on your experience??

replies.author: Loose-Object-8913
replies.body:
Pretty selective from what I’ve seen.

In clean trends it can take multiple entries (especially with pyramiding), but in messy weeks it might barely trade at all.

So not really a fixed frequency. It’s based on conditions more than anything.

comments.author: ddchbr
comments.body: How do you filter out chop / determine a clean/clear trend?

replies.author: Loose-Object-8913
replies.body:
It’s not just one thing — it’s more a combination of filters working together.

Mainly looking at:
- higher timeframe direction (EMA + general structure)
- momentum (ADX helps avoid low energy markets)
- how price is behaving around pullbacks vs just chopping sideways

If those don’t align, it just doesn’t trade.

So I’m not really trying to label “chop” directly , it just naturally gets filtered out when structure + momentum aren’t there.

Still refining it, but that’s the general idea.
